info.title: Motion Freight Router API
info.description: Drop-in replacement for Google Routes API with segment-level physics enrichment for heavy freight. Provides both a Google-compatible synchronous endpoint and stateful route management endpoints.

get.description: Returns the health status of the API.

post.description: Drop-in replacement for the Google Routes API. Returns the standard response with additional physics enrichment per step.

parameters.name: X-Goog-FieldMask
parameters.description: Accepted for Google compatibility but ignored. The full response is always returned.

with_vehicle_and_driver.summary: Route with vehicle spec and driver state

200.description: Successfully computed route(s). The response matches the Google Routes API `computeRoutes` response shape exactly, with an additional `enrichment` field on each step.

X-Route-Id.description: The internal route resource ID. Use this to access the route via the `/v1/routes/{id}` endpoint.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g. unsupported waypoint type, missing location)

error.message: Address and placeId waypoints are not supported; use latLng coordinates.
No route could be found between the given locations.

post.description: Creates a route resource with physics enrichment.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique key to ensure idempotent route creation. If a request with the same key was already processed, the cached response is returned. If the same key is reused with a different request body, a 422 error is returned.

200.description: Route computed synchronously (fast path). Status is `complete` with full route data.

202.description: Route accepted for async processing (slow path). Poll `GET /v1/routes/{id}` for completion.

409.description: Idempotency key lock contention. Retry after the specified delay.

422.description: Idempotency key reused with a different request body, or routing error

get.description: Retrieve a route resource by its ID. Completed routes expire after 6 hours.

parameters.name: route_id
parameters.description: The unique route resource ID (prefixed with `route_`)

delete.description: Permanently deletes a route and its associated telemetry data.

parameters.name: route_id
parameters.description: The unique route resource ID (prefixed with `route_`)

get.description: Returns paginated segment-level physics data (grade, curvature, fuel burn, stress) for a completed route.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor (segment index offset). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of segments to return per page. Defaults to 50, max 200.

404.description: Route not found or still processing

error.message: Route is still processing. Poll GET /v1/routes/{route_id} until status is 'complete'.

patch.description: Updates driver fatigue, attention, and stress parameters for a route, then re-computes all enrichment scores.

200.description: Driver state updated and enrichment re-scored

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the update

error.message: Cannot update driver state while route is processing.
Cannot update driver state for a failed route.

Waypoint.description: A waypoint for route computation. Only `location` with `latLng` is supported. `placeId` and `address` are not supported and will return an error.

location.description: Geographic location (required — must use latLng coordinates)

placeId.description: **Not supported.** Providing this will return an error. Use `location` with `latLng` instead.

address.description: **Not supported.** Providing this will return an error. Use `location` with `latLng` instead.

via.description: If true, the waypoint is a pass-through point (no stop). If false, the waypoint is a stop.

VehicleSpec.description: Vehicle specification for physics-based enrichment. All fields are optional — unset fields fall back to the preset for the given `type`.

type.description: Vehicle preset type. Defaults to SEMI_TRAILER.

idleFuelLitersPerHour.description: Fuel consumption at idle in liters per hour

DriverState.description: Driver state parameters that affect stress scoring and enrichment

fatigueScore.description: Driver fatigue level (0 = fully rested, 1 = maximum fatigue)

attentionScore.description: Driver attention level (0 = no attention, 1 = fully attentive)

stressLevel.description: Current driver stress level (0 = no stress, 1 = maximum stress)

DriverStateUpdate.description: Partial update for driver state. Only provided fields are updated.

ComputeRoutesRequest.description: Request body for route computation. Accepts all Google Routes API fields — unsupported fields are silently ignored.

travelMode.description: Travel mode. Only `DRIVE` is supported (truck routing).

routingPreference.description: Routing preference. Traffic is not currently modeled.

departureTime.description: Departure time in ISO 8601 UTC format. Affects school zone detection and time-based routing.

computeAlternativeRoutes.description: When true and no intermediate waypoints, returns up to 3 alternative routes.

vehicleSpec.description: Vehicle specification for physics enrichment. Defaults to SEMI_TRAILER preset if omitted.

driverState.description: Current driver state for stress scoring. Defaults to fully rested if omitted.

routingProfile.description: Routing profile that adjusts enrichment weights

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL for async route completion notifications. Only used by `POST /v1/routes`.

encodedPolyline.description: Encoded polyline string at precision 5 (Google standard)

NavigationInstruction.description: Navigation instruction for a route step

StepEnrichment.description: Physics-based enrichment data for a route step. This is the extension data that rides alongside Google's standard step fields.

gradePercent.description: Road grade as a percentage (positive = uphill, negative = downhill)

curvatureDegreesPerKm.description: Road curvature in degrees of bearing change per kilometer

fuelBurnLiters.description: Estimated fuel burn for this step in liters

stressFactor.description: Computed stress factor for this step (higher = more stressful)

zoneFlags.description: Active zone flags for this step

degradedFields.description: List of fields that could not be computed (e.g. elevation unavailable)

RouteLegStep.description: A step within a route leg. Matches Google's RouteLegStep with an additional `enrichment` field.

RouteLeg.description: A leg of a route (between two consecutive stops). Matches Google's RouteLeg.

Viewport.description: A bounding box for the route

Route.description: A computed route. Matches Google's Route object with enrichment on each step.

routeLabels.description: Route labels. Primary route is `DEFAULT_ROUTE`, alternatives are `DEFAULT_ROUTE_ALTERNATE`.

GoogleComputeRoutesResponse.description: Response from the Google-compatible endpoint. Matches Google's `computeRoutes` response exactly with no extra top-level fields.

RouteResource.description: A route resource. Used by the `/v1/routes` endpoint family.

status.description:
Current status of the route computation.

- `processing` — route is still being computed (routes array is empty)
- `complete` — route computation finished (routes array is populated)
- `failed` — route computation failed (error object is populated)

TelemetrySegment.description: Telemetry data for a single route segment

TelemetrySummary.description: Aggregated telemetry summary for the entire route

totalFuelBurnLiters.description: Total estimated fuel burn in liters

maxGradePercent.description: Maximum grade percentage on the route

schoolZoneCount.description: Number of segments passing through school zones

residentialSegmentCount.description: Number of segments in residential areas

overallStressScore.description: Overall stress score for the route (0-1)

TelemetryResponse.description: Full telemetry response with summary and per-segment data

vehicleSpec.description: Resolved vehicle specification used for computation

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page of segments

param.description: The parameter that caused the error

WebhookEvent.description: Webhook event payload sent when a route computation completes or fails

tags.name: Google-Compatible
tags.description: Drop-in replacement for Google Routes API. Same request/response format.

tags.name: Routes
tags.description: Stateful route management with sync/async computation.

tags.name: Telemetry
tags.description: Segment-level physics telemetry data (grade, curvature, fuel burn, stress).

tags.name: Driver State
tags.description: Update driver state and trigger enrichment re-scoring.
